data.title: Intel--TSMC Trade?Secret Bombshell Erupts Just as Nvidia Earnings Hit the Spotlight
data.description:
Investors watching Intel (INTC, Financial) this week are getting a plot twist just as the entire semiconductor space positions for Nvidia's earnings. Intel pushed back against allegations from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM, Financial) that former senior vice president Wei-Jen Lo may have leaked confidential information after joining the U.S. chipmaker in October. In an emailed statement, Intel said it has no reason to believe the claims have merit and reiterated that its policies prohibit the transfer or use of any third-party intellectual property. The company also described Lo—who returned after an earlier 18-year stint at Intel and a 21-year run at TSMC—as a respected industry figure with a long track record of technical leadership.

TSMC has taken a different view, filing a lawsuit in Taiwan's Intellectual Property and Commercial Court and arguing there is a high probability Lo could have used, disclosed or transferred its trade secrets. Taiwan's economy ministry signaled it will cooperate on determining whether the case touches on core technologies or possible National Security Act violations. Lo did not respond to comment requests, and Intel framed the situation as part of normal talent movement in a globally competitive industry, though the timing could be raising eyebrows across the market.

All of this lands as AMD (AMD, Financial), Intel and TSMC brace for Nvidia's (NVDA, Financial) results—an event that could be setting the tone for how investors position around advanced manufacturing, supply visibility and sector leadership. With Lo having contributed to TSMC's push into 5-nm, 3-nm and 2-nm mass production, the dispute introduces an added layer of uncertainty at a moment when traders are already watching every signal from Nvidia for clues on demand trajectory and competitive intensity.

data.title: ASML Stock Climbs After Morgan Stanley Tags It a 'Top Pick' for 2026
data.description:
ASML (ASML, Financial) shares climbed about 4% on Wednesday U.S trading after Morgan Stanley raised its price target to €1,000 from €975 and named Dutch chip-equipment maker its "Top Pick" in European semiconductors.

The bank says demand is firming across memory and logic as DRAM producers shift to newer nodes, which should increase lithography layers and drive EUV intensity into fiscal 2026. Analyst Lee Simpson cited the lift from DRAM transitions and said higher EUV sales alongside a richer product mix may help sustain margins.

Morgan Stanley models a 52.3% gross margin for 2026, about 40 basis points below last year, and expects China demand to fall roughly 15%, less severe than prior company guidance. The team also flagged upside risks from DRAM and foundry spending tied to AI infrastructure and said recent share weakness offers an "attractive entry point."

The bank reiterated an Overweight rating and noted Nvidia (NVDA, Financial)'s robust Blackwell demand as a positive read-through for ASML's tool cycles. Investors will watch order visibility and momentum from Samsung and SK Hynix as the key near-term catalyst.

data.title:
Palantir: The Most Expensive Stock in History Has Big Problems
        (Status: Pending)
data.description:
Palantir Technologies (PLTR, Financial) faces fresh scrutiny as investors digest a mix of strong sales and heavy insider selling. Although the company posted solid growth, revenue jumped about 63% to roughly $1.2 billion in the most recent quarter, yet the stock trades with an unusually rich valuation for a company of its size.

CEO Alex Karp publicly criticized short sellers on CNBC after the earnings release, which drew attention because he and other executives have been filing plans to sell shares. In early November, Karp filed to sell roughly 585,000 shares at about $163.99 per share, part of a broader pattern of sales that topped $200 million in recent filings. Executives say many transactions run through prearranged 10b5-1 plans, used for diversification and tax planning, but investors naturally weigh those sales against public rhetoric.

Market metrics raise questions. With roughly 2.57 billion shares outstanding and a market cap that exceeded $500 billion at recent peaks, Palantir traded at very high multiples compared with traditional peers. Analysts and data providers point out forward multiples that require sustained above-average growth to justify current prices.

Competition adds another layer. Snowflake (SNOW, Financial) and other data and AI firms have shown that market sentiment can shift quickly, and private players such as Databricks keep the competitive landscape dynamic.

Palantir is continuing to lose a fast-expanding business that has strong government and corporate integrations. However, when you consider investing, you must be on the lookout for insider selling, stock-based compensation that waters the shareholders down, and the likelihood of the current projected growth exceeding the already sky-high expectations already embedded in the shares.

In the meantime, the company's performance and leadership position put it at a stock worth maintaining a watch in lieu of merely taking part in the hype.

data.title: Cathie Wood Buying Dip in CoreWeave After 45% Crash -- Should You?
data.description:
Ark Invest boosted its exposure to CoreWeave (CRWV, Financial) on Monday, stepping in after the AI-infrastructure provider's shares retreated sharply over the past month.

The bulk of the buying occurred in the ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK, Financial), which added 396,178 shares. Following the move, ARKK's stake is valued at roughly $52.4 million, representing about 0.7% of the fund.

The ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW, Financial) increased its position as well, acquiring 41,167 shares. ARKW now holds approximately $35.8 million in CoreWeave, making the stock its 19th-largest weight at about 1.7% of assets.The investment firm purchased a combined 437,345 CoreWeave (CRWV, Financial) shares across two of its active ETFs.

CoreWeave has lost close to 45% over the last month amid cooling sentiment toward high-growth AI names. Ark has historically used sharp drawdowns to accumulate positions it views as tied to long-term technology themes, including advanced compute infrastructure and cloud-based acceleration services.

Is CRWV Stock a Buy?

Based on the one year price targets offered by 26 analysts, the average target price for CoreWeave Inc is $131.80 with a high estimate of $234.00 and a low estimate of $36.00. The average target implies a upside of +84.87% from the current price of $71.29.

Based on the consensus recommendation from 31 brokerage firms, CoreWeave Inc's (CRWV, Financial) average brokerage recommendation is currently 2.5, indicating a "Outperform" status. The rating scale ranges from 1 to 5, where 1 signifies Strong Buy, and 5 denotes Sell.

data.title: Tesla Europe Sales Crash 48% -- BYD Triples Sales in Aggressive Takeover
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA, Financial) Europe registrations nearly halved in October, falling 48.5% to 6,964 units.

In contrast, Chinese rival BYD (1211) sold 17,470 cars in the region in October, up about 207% year-on-year, lifting its market share to roughly 1.6% while Tesla's share slipped to 0.6%.

Overall passenger car registrations rose 4.9% to about 1.09 million units, with hybrid electric vehicles remaining the largest segment.

Market observers say BYD's broader model range, including plug-in hybrids, has helped it sidestep some tariff pressure and win buyers across Europe.

Analysts add Tesla's weak European momentum reflects tougher competition and consumer headwinds tied to CEO Elon Musk's public profile.

The outcome demonstrates the potential of the rapid EV market in Europe to turn within a short time. Dealers and investors will continue monitoring the question of whether the BYD boom will continue to last to Q4 and whether Tesla will be capable of stabilizing demand with new product variations.

Regulators will watch production plans and tariffs as local manufacturing and pricing strategies could reshape European EV competition next year.

data.title:
Nvidia Slips After Report of Meta Turning to Google for Billions in AI Chips
        (Status: Pending)
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA, Financial) shares fell about 4% in early Tuesday trading after reports that Meta (META) is in talks to use Google's tensor processing units, or TPUs, in data centers starting in 2027.

The Information first reported the discussions, and Bloomberg later carried the story, saying Meta may also rent TPUs from Alphabet's cloud unit next year.

Market participants said such a shift could offer a viable alternative to Nvidia's chips, which are the default choice for many large language model developers and cloud customers.

Alphabet (GOOGL) shares jumped more than 2% in early trading, while related suppliers in Asia climbed as investors priced possible gains for Google's cloud and chip business.

Google previously agreed to supply up to 1 million TPUs to Anthropic PBC, a deal analysts say validates third-party demand for the technology.

Representatives for Meta declined to comment, and Google did not immediately respond to requests for comment.

data.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) Prices €2.1 Billion Euro-Denominated Notes
data.description:
Key Takeaways:

  • Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO, Financial) has priced €2.1 billion in euro-denominated notes to support corporate activities.
  • The company operates in the healthcare sector, focusing on scientific instruments and laboratory equipment.
  • Thermo Fisher's financial health is robust, with a strong Altman Z-Score and manageable debt levels.

Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) has announced the pricing of €2.1 billion in euro-denominated notes through its finance subsidiary. The issuance includes €1 billion in floating rate senior notes due 2027 and €1.1 billion in fixed rate senior notes with a 3.628% interest rate due 2035. Closing is anticipated around December 1, 2025, subject to customary conditions. The proceeds will support general corporate activities such as acquisitions, debt repayment, and working capital, with potential temporary investment in short-term assets.

Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc is a leading player in the healthcare sector, specializing in the sale of scientific instruments, laboratory equipment, diagnostics consumables, and life science reagents. The company operates through four segments: analytical technologies, specialty diagnostic products, life science solutions, and lab products and services. With a market capitalization of approximately $220.19 billion, Thermo Fisher is well-positioned within the medical diagnostics and research industry.

Financial Health Analysis

Thermo Fisher's financial performance is characterized by steady revenue growth and robust profitability metrics. The company reported a revenue of $43.735 billion, with a three-year revenue growth rate of 4.3%. Despite a slight decline in gross margin to 41.27%, the company maintains a healthy operating margin of 18.41% and a net margin of 15.02%.

On the balance sheet front, Thermo Fisher exhibits financial strength with a current ratio of 1.5 and a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.7. The company's Altman Z-Score of 4.11 indicates strong financial health, while the Beneish M-Score of -2.41 suggests it is unlikely to be a financial manipulator.

Valuation & Market Sentiment

Thermo Fisher's valuation metrics reflect its market positioning. The company's P/E ratio stands at 33.86, close to its one-year high, while the P/S ratio is 5.13, nearing a three-year high. The P/B ratio of 4.32 also indicates a valuation close to historical peaks.

Analyst sentiment remains positive, with a target price of $613.06 and a recommendation score of 2. Institutional ownership is high at 89.35%, reflecting confidence in the company's prospects. However, insider activity shows a trend of selling, with eight insider sell transactions in the past three months.

Risk Assessment

Thermo Fisher's financial health is supported by strong grades in financial strength, as evidenced by its high Altman Z-Score. However, sector-specific risks in the healthcare industry, such as regulatory changes and competitive pressures, remain pertinent. The company's beta of 1.04 suggests moderate volatility, aligning with its classification as a slow-growth stock.

In conclusion, Thermo Fisher Scientific's recent debt issuance is a strategic move to bolster its financial flexibility for future growth initiatives. The company's robust financial metrics and market positioning provide a solid foundation, although investors should remain vigilant of insider selling trends and sector-specific risks.

data.title: BHP Just Blinked: The $60 Billion Copper War Takes a Shocking Turn
data.description:
BHP's (BHP, Financial) latest move landed like a plot twist investors didn't see coming. After holding preliminary discussions with Anglo American in recent days, the miner said it is no longer considering a combination, effectively abandoning a short-lived attempt to interrupt Anglo's planned merger with Teck Resources. People familiar with the matter said Anglo reviewed the approach and rejected it, concluding it was not superior to the Teck tie-up already headed for a December 9 shareholder vote. The timing raised eyebrows: this was BHP's second overture in two years, following a failed 2024 proposal that would have required Anglo to partially break itself up, even as the two copper-rich targets prepare to create a company worth more than $60 billion.

Investors spent Monday trying to interpret what the retreat could mean for a sector where copper scarcity and electrification-driven demand keep raising the stakes. BHP shares initially jumped as much as 1.3% in Sydney before fading to a 0.1% gain near the close, reflecting a market still digesting the pivot. Dylan Kelly at Terra Capital said some shareholders were possibly surprised because CEO Mike Henry had spent months emphasizing discipline around existing assets. Meanwhile, Anglo shares have gained 11% in London since BHP's previous bid ended, even as BHP's have drifted lower, underscoring a backdrop where copper miners with long-life assets have become both rare and expensive. Van Eck's Jamie Hannah suggested the attempt was always going to be difficult in this environment rather than a straightforward strategic miss.

Now the focus moves back to BHP's own copper portfolio and the decisive December 9 vote that will determine whether Anglo and Teck proceed with their merger. Glyn Lawcock at Barrenjoey said the miner will likely need to lean harder into its existing growth engines, including Escondida in Chile, the Vicuna venture in Argentina, and operations in South Australia. BHP said the Anglo combination could have offered strong strategic merits but reiterated confidence in the potential of its organic growth strategy. Lazard, UBS, and Barclays advised BHP on the now-abandoned approach, while the Anglo-Teck deal remains subject to regulatory approvals in China, the US, and Canada—setting up a consequential stretch for one of the copper market's most closely watched consolidation stories.

data.title: Alphabet hits record high as AI momentum accelerates
data.description:
Alphabet (GOOGL, Financials) surged to a record close of $299.66 as investors responded to stronger earnings, improving cloud demand and continued progress in artificial intelligence development. The stock has gained about 59% this year, with recent momentum driven by optimism around the company's expanding AI capabilities.

Much of the market enthusiasm stems from Gemini 3, Alphabet's latest AI model. Early testing indicates the system outperformed a competing model by 17% in code generation and instruction handling, while analysts highlighted faster reasoning and improved responsiveness.

The company also introduced a new version of its Tensor Processing Unit designed to handle larger AI workloads with far greater energy efficiency, a move expected to attract more enterprise cloud customers.

Waymo, Alphabet's autonomous vehicle arm, added to the positive sentiment with plans to expand testing of its ride-hailing service in three more U.S. cities, broadening potential long-term revenue opportunities beyond advertising and cloud services.

Alphabet's most recent quarterly results supported the rally. Earnings of $2.87 per share topped expectations, and revenue rose 16% to $102.35 billion
